content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
September 1,
1992
William Gaistan,
City Attorney
City of Antioch
P.O.
Box 130
Antioch,
CA
94509—0504
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-554
Dear Mr.
Gaistan:
You have requested advice concerning your reporting
obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions
of the Political Reform Act.’!
As city attorney for the City of Antioch,
you must report on
your statement of economic interests
(Form 721) gifts that
aggregate $50 or more from a single source.
You have asked
whether you are required to disclose receiving a gift of baseball
tickets if you reimburse the donor the face value of the tickets.
Regulation 18726.1(b) (4)
states that if an official
reimburses the donor within 30 days of receipt of a gift, the
official has not received a gift.
Therefore, if within 30 days of
receipt you reimburse the donor for the full price of the baseball
tickets,
you are not required to disclose receipt of the tickets
on your statement of economic interests.
If you have any questions or need further assistance, please
contact me at
(916)
322—5662.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
by:
Colleen McGee
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
“Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000,
et
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 j Street, Suite 800
•
P.O. Box 807
•
Sacramento, CA
95812
•
(916) 322-5660

sections.question: whether you are required to disclose receiving a gift of baseball tickets if you reimburse the donor the face value of the tickets
sections.conclusion: if within 30 days of receipt you reimburse the donor for the full price of the baseball tickets, you are not required to disclose receipt of the tickets on your statement of economic interests
sections.question_synthetic: Must a city attorney disclose receipt of baseball tickets on their statement of economic interests if they reimburse the donor for the full face value of the tickets within 30 days?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: No disclosure is required if the official reimburses the donor for the full price of the gift within 30 days of receipt, as Regulation 18726.1(b)(4) states that reimbursement within 30 days means the official has not received a gift.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear question and conclusion sections. Document addressed to William Gaistan, City Attorney for City of Antioch. Response provided by Scott Hallabrin, Acting General Counsel, with technical assistance from Colleen McGee.

QUESTION: whether you are required to disclose receiving a gift of baseball tickets if you reimburse the donor the face value of the tickets

CONCLUSION: if within 30 days of receipt you reimburse the donor for the full price of the baseball tickets, you are not required to disclose receipt of the tickets on your statement of economic interests
embedding.first_500_words: California Fair Political Practices Commission September 1, 1992 William Gaistan, City Attorney City of Antioch P.O. Box 130 Antioch, CA 94509—0504 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-92-554 Dear Mr. Gaistan: You have requested advice concerning your reporting obligations under the conflict of interest disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.’! As city attorney for the City of Antioch, you must report on your statement of economic interests (Form 721) gifts that aggregate $50 or more from a single source. You have asked whether you are required to disclose receiving a gift of baseball tickets if you reimburse the donor the face value of the tickets. Regulation 18726.1(b) (4) states that if an official reimburses the donor within 30 days of receipt of a gift, the official has not received a gift. Therefore, if within 30 days of receipt you reimburse the donor for the full price of the baseball tickets, you are not required to disclose receipt of the tickets on your statement of economic interests. If you have any questions or need further assistance, please contact me at (916) 322—5662. Sincerely, Scott Hallabrin Acting General Counsel by: Colleen McGee Political Reform Consultant Technical Assistance Division “Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95812 • (916) 322-5660
embedding.summary: FPPC advises that a city attorney is not required to disclose baseball tickets on their statement of economic interests if they reimburse the donor for the full face value within 30 days of receipt. This is based on Regulation 18726.1(b)(4), which provides that reimbursement within 30 days means the gift was not received.
